text: Waters, which in that country are very scarce.<hr>Milk. Septuagint, "fat." (Calmet)<hr>In the western Churches, wine and honey were given to the new baptized, Isaias 7:15. (St. Jerome)<hr>Christ invites all to come to him, John 4:14,. and 7:37. The establishment of the Church is described under the figure of the return from Babylon. (Calmet)<hr>Grace is offered to all. But only those are justified who thirst, and do their best, Matthew 5:6. (Worthington)
David. I will be equally faithful to you, Psalm 88:29. Septuagint, "the holy things of David faithful," Acts 13:34. The resurrection of Christ fully accomplished the promise made to David.
Him. David, who continually proclaimed the divine mercies, (Psalm 17:1.) or rather Christ; though Grotius alone would refer it to Jeremias. (Calmet)
Not with approbation. (Haydock)<hr>The Gentiles are converted.
Near. He will shortly (Calmet) turn to the Gentiles, John 12:35., and Acts 13:46. (Haydock)
Way. This is a necessary preliminary to God's service. (Worthington)
My ways. I am not vindictive, but require a sincere conversion. (Calmet)<hr>We cannot serve both God and the world. We must therefore adhere to the former. (Worthington)
Sent it. I will assuredly bring you from Babylon; and the rain shall sooner return upwards than I will break my promise.
Peace, by strangers, Isaias 49:22.<hr>Hands, for joy. (Calmet)<hr>Ipsa sonant arbusta Deus Deus ille, Menacla. (Virgil, Eclogues v.)
Myrtle-tree. Instead of the wicked, the just shall be seen. (Chaldean)<hr>Away. The conversion and sanctity of the Gentiles shall be a trophy to the Lord. (Menochius)
